title: ENERTRAG Joins Polish Consortium to Build Hydrogen Tech Center in Lipnik
content: German renewable energy company ENERTRAG has joined forces with the West Pomeranian University of Technology in Szczecin (ZUT) and Locus-Tech to develop a major green hydrogen hub in Lipnik, near Stargard, Poland. The strategic partnership, which formally included ENERTRAG as of June 24, 2025, aims to leverage the companyâ€™s deep expertise in green hydrogen and Power-to-X technologies. The project, initially launched in December 2024, will begin with a 20 MW green hydrogen production facility, with plans to scale up to 100 MW. This initiative not only strengthens regional energy resilience but also supports broader EU decarbonization efforts and fosters cross-border collaboration in renewable hydrogen. Beyond infrastructure, the Lipnik Hydrogen Technology Centre will serve as a platform for technology transfer, education, and the advancement of decentralized energy solutions, positioning the region as a key player in Europeâ€™s clean energy transition.
author: Analysts Account
